text.title: A11y No-Regression PR Gate Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed when applying A11y No-Regression PR Gate. Each ties a symptom to a root cause and a concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Waiver-everything — team uses the waiver file as a bypass instead of a temporary exception. Waivers accumulate without resolution.
antipattern.root-cause: `.a11y-waivers.json` length > 30 entries OR > 20% of waivers older than 60 days without ticket activity
antipattern.fix: Pause waiver acceptance. Audit each waiver: fix, escalate to product for "won't fix" decision with documented business reason, or roll into the next release plan. Tighten reviewer policy: only a11y lead can approve waivers, not the PR author or a peer dev.

antipattern.symptom: Baseline drift — baseline gets quietly updated to include new violations, hiding regressions.
antipattern.root-cause: git log on baseline file shows diff with new violations added in the same commit as feature code (instead of after a fix-PR merge)
antipattern.fix: Revert the baseline. Require all baseline updates to come from a dedicated `chore(a11y): rebase baseline after fix #X` PR that lands AFTER the fix. Add a CODEOWNERS rule: baseline file requires a11y lead review.

antipattern.symptom: Flaky rules — axe / pa11y returns intermittent violations due to timing, animation, or third-party widgets, eroding trust in the gate.
antipattern.root-cause: same rule reports violation in >= 30% of runs and no violation in >= 30% of runs, on unchanged code (sampled over 20 runs)
antipattern.fix: Investigate root cause: usually animation timing or async-load. Fix the test setup (wait for stable DOM, disable animations during scan). If the rule is genuinely flaky for the framework (some axe rules are known flaky for SPA hydration), disable that rule globally with a documented exception and a tracking ticket.

antipattern.symptom: Route map drift — gate scans wrong routes because route map is stale. A new page ships without a11y coverage.
antipattern.fix: Make route map auto-generation part of CI: a separate check verifies route map matches the app. If they diverge, fail the check until route map is updated. Add a "new route added" PR template item.

antipattern.symptom: Performance erosion — gate scan grew from 30 sec to 5+ minutes as routes were added, slowing the merge queue.
antipattern.root-cause: median gate run duration over last 30 days > 180 sec OR p95 > 360 sec
antipattern.fix: Parallelize scans across routes (axe-core supports this). Restrict per-PR scan to changed routes (r4) instead of all routes. Move full-site scan to nightly schedule. If still slow, sub-sample within a route to top-level components only.

antipattern.symptom: Severity mis-classification — a violation flagged as "moderate" by axe is actually critical for the product (e.g., the company's primary CTA is unreachable by keyboard).
antipattern.root-cause: customer-reported a11y issue traces back to a violation that the gate reported as moderate/minor and allowed
antipattern.fix: Override the default severity for the specific selector / route in a `.a11y/severity-overrides.json` file. Don't broadly upgrade the rule severity globally — it'll create new noise. Document the override with rationale.
